title: Balogh–Clemen–Lavrov–Lidický–Pfender Conjecture
statement_text: For every $r \geq 2$ there is a $\delta_r > 0$ such that the following holds: If $G$ is a $K_{r+1}$-free graph on $n$ vertices with $e(G) \geq t_r(n) - \delta_r n^2$ edges, then there is a pentagonal Turán graph $G^*$ on $n$ vertices with $e(G^*) \geq e(G)$ and $D_r(G^*) \geq D_r(G)$.
context_text: Let $f_r(n,t)$ be the smallest number such that any $K_{r+1}$-free graph $G$ with at least $t_r(n)-t$ edges can be made $r$-partite by deleting at most $f_r(n,t)$ edges. Balogh, Clemen, Lavrov, Lidický and Pfender determined $f_r(n,t)$ asymptotically and conjectured that its exact value, for $t$ sufficiently small relative to $n^2$, is witnessed by a pentagonal Turán graph — a blowup of $C_5$ embedded in a complete $(r-1)$-partite host graph with balanced part sizes. The conjecture subsumes and generalises the triangle-free case resolved by Erdős, Győri and Simonovits.
attributed_to: Balogh, Clemen, Lavrov, Lidický, and Pfender
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full as Theorem 1.2: for every $r \geq 2$ there exists $\delta_r > 0$ such that among all $K_{r+1}$-free $n$-vertex graphs with at least $t_r(n) - \delta_r n^2$ edges, a pentagonal Turán graph simultaneously achieves the maximum edge count and the maximum $r$-partite distance $D_r$.
notes: The conjecture is stated in prose in the introduction ('Balogh, Clemen, Lavrov, Lidický and Pfender [5] conjectured that $f_r(n,t)$ is witnessed by a pentagonal Turán graph if $t$ is small enough'); the precise formal statement is reconstructed from the paper's Theorem 1.2, which is explicitly identified as the proved version of the conjecture. Section 5 ('open problems') is not present in the PDF extraction, so any additional items stated there could not be captured.
